VAL-INSTALL-001.evidence: Simulated full github: shortcut flow (git clone → npm install → prepare → npm pack → npm i -g tarball) in clean environment without global TypeScript. Verified: `which tsc` exits 1, `npm install` triggers prepare script which uses `node_modules/.bin/tsc -p tsconfig.build.json` (commit 891ee57 fix), build succeeds (exit 0), `npm pack` creates mors-0.1.0.tgz, global install from tarball succeeds (exit 0), `mors --version` outputs 'mors 0.1.0' (exit 0). No manual build step was required. The prepare script correctly resolves tsc via explicit local path `node_modules/.bin/tsc`.

VAL-INSTALL-007.evidence: npm GitHub shortcut path: Full git-clone + prepare + pack + global-install flow succeeds without global TypeScript (exit 0 at every step), `mors --version` outputs 'mors 0.1.0' (exit 0). Homebrew formula path: Formula/mors.rb passes ruby syntax check (exit 0), brew style check (0 offenses, exit 0), CLI runtime proof via `node dist/index.js --version` outputs 'mors 0.1.0' (exit 0). Both distribution channels produce working CLI entrypoints. Note: actual `brew install` from formula requires npm registry publication for SHA256 match; formula structure and runtime CLI are validated.

VAL-CROSS-007.evidence: Full first-run operational flow from simulated GitHub shortcut install: (1) git clone + npm install + prepare succeeds without global tsc, (2) npm pack + global install to isolated prefix succeeds (exit 0), (3) `mors --version` outputs 'mors 0.1.0' (exit 0), (4) `MORS_CONFIG_DIR=<temp> mors init --json` outputs initialized status with fingerprint (exit 0), (5) `MORS_CONFIG_DIR=<temp> mors inbox --json` outputs count:0 messages (exit 0), (6) `MORS_CONFIG_DIR=<temp> mors send --to agent-b --body 'Hello from install test' --json` outputs sent status with message ID (exit 0), (7) `MORS_CONFIG_DIR=<temp> mors inbox --json` outputs count:1 with sent message (exit 0). Complete first-run lifecycle verified.
